title: Happy Birthday, Jaanu 💖
paragraphs: My Babydu. Today is your day, and I just want you to know — from the very bottom of my heart, from the deepest and most real part of me — that I love you. I love you so much that words almost feel too small, too ordinary, too plain to carry the weight of everything I feel for you. But I'm going to try anyway, because you deserve every single word, every single sentence, every single thought I could ever put together — and then some.

You came into my life and everything just… shifted. Like the world quietly rearranged itself to make more sense. Like colours got a little brighter, like songs got a little sweeter, like even the most ordinary Tuesday started feeling like something worth waking up for. That's what you do to me, my Pengu. You make everything feel warmer. You make everything feel like home.

I don't even know where to begin when I think about how much I love you, Babydu. I love you in the mornings when you're still half asleep and the world hasn't fully started yet. I love you in the evenings when we're just talking about nothing and somehow it becomes everything. I love you in the quiet moments and the loud ones, in the silly ones and the serious ones, in every version of every day we've shared together. You are my favourite part of all of it.

My Bondu, you are the kind of person that people spend their whole lives hoping to find. You are so genuinely, effortlessly, beautifully kind. Not the surface-level kind that people perform for others — but the deep, real kind that comes from the very core of who you are. You are kind in the way you listen. Kind in the way you speak. Kind in the way you make people around you feel seen and heard and valued. You have this warmth about you that I don't think you even fully realize, and it is one of the most extraordinary things about you.

And sweet — god, Bangari, you are the sweetest person I have ever known. Not in a small way. In the biggest, most overwhelming way. You do the little things that most people don't even notice — but I notice. I always notice. The way you remember the things I say. The way you check on me without me even having to ask. The way you make me feel like I matter, like I'm worth caring about, like I'm seen — truly seen — by you. That is rare. That is so incredibly rare. And I will never stop being grateful for it.

You understand me, my Pengu, in ways I didn't even know I needed to be understood. There are parts of me I've never been able to explain to anyone else, feelings I've struggled to put into words, moments where I didn't even understand myself — and somehow, you just got it. You never made me feel judged. You never made me feel like too much or not enough. You just showed up, every single time, with that gentle heart of yours, and you made space for all of me. That is the greatest gift anyone has ever given me.

I love you, my Babydu, for every conversation we've ever had. For every laugh we've shared. For every moment where you looked at me and I felt like the luckiest person alive. I love you for the way your eyes light up when you're excited about something. I love you for the way you laugh — genuinely, freely, fully — like you're not holding anything back. I love you for the way you love. Because you love with everything you have, and that is something I see clearly, every single day.

Happy Birthday, my Bondu. Today is all about you. Today the world should pause for a second and celebrate the fact that you exist, that you are here, that you are you — because the world is genuinely, undeniably better with you in it. You make it softer. You make it kinder. You make it more beautiful just by being a part of it.

I want you to know, on this birthday, that you are deeply loved. Not just by me — though I love you more than I could ever properly explain — but you are loved by everyone whose life you've touched. You leave something good behind everywhere you go. A warmth. A kindness. A little piece of you that stays with people long after you've walked away. That is who you are, Bangari. That is what you do. And it is everything.

I think about the future and I see you in all of it. Every version of what comes next, every plan, every dream, every ordinary Wednesday and every extraordinary moment — you're in all of them. Because I can't imagine doing any of this without you. I don't want to. You are not just someone I love — you are someone I choose, every single day, without hesitation, without doubt, without question.

My Pengu, you deserve a birthday that feels as magical as you are. You deserve to be celebrated and spoiled and reminded of your worth in every possible way. You deserve flowers and music and laughter and all the love in the world wrapped up and placed right in your hands. You deserve every good thing. Every single one. And I hope today feels like that — like the universe finally got its act together and decided to give you everything you've always deserved.

I love you, Babydu. I love you when you're happy and when you're sad. I love you when you're being the sweetest person alive and when you're being my little Bondu who makes me smile even on the hardest days. I love all the versions of you — every single one. There is not a version of you that I wouldn't choose. There is not a moment where I look at you and feel anything less than completely, overwhelmingly full of love for you.

You have this way of making me feel calm, Bangari. Like no matter what's happening, no matter how loud or messy or uncertain life gets — when I'm with you, everything settles. You are my calm. You are the place I come back to. You are the person I want to tell everything to — the good things first, because I know you'll be just as happy about them as I am, and the hard things too, because I know you'll sit with me through them and never once make me feel alone.

My Bondu, thank you. Thank you for being you. Thank you for choosing me. Thank you for every moment of kindness, every word of understanding, every time you showed up when I needed you. Thank you for the laughter and the comfort and the warmth and the love. Thank you for being the kind of person who makes everyone around her better, softer, more hopeful. Thank you for being mine.

On this birthday, I just want you to feel it — really feel it — how loved you are. Not just today, not just in this moment, but always. Constantly. Completely. You are so deeply loved, my Bangari. By me, more than anything, more than I know how to say, more than these words can hold.
Happy Birthday, my Pengu. My Bondu. My Babydu. My Bangari. My love. My favourite person in the entire world.
I love you. I love you. I love you.
